extraction.one_line_summary: MAIDA Gino attacked 3.3km from finish on 15km climb, held off BERNAL's late pressure by 12 seconds for maiden pro win.
tactical_patterns: INEOS Grenadiers shelled maglia rosa FILIPPO on descent crosswinds before final climb, revealing pre-climb positioning vulnerability.
BAHRAIN launches MAIDA solo at 3.3km rather than summit finish, exploiting GC group hesitation on long gradual climb.
BERNAL waits until final kilometer to chase solo leader rather than organizing pursuit, conceding 12 seconds tactically.
GC group fragments with small time gaps throughout top-10, suggesting 15km gradual climb lacks selection power for strongest climbers.

rider_signals.VALTER Attila: positive: 22-year-old took maglia rosa as first Hungarian ever, positioned within 11 seconds of serious GC contenders
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: negative: maglia rosa dropped on descent before final climb by INEOS crosswind acceleration, exposed as time-trial specialist
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: finished second but failed to organize chase of solo attacker until final kilometer, questionable race management
rider_signals.MAIDA Gino: positive: 24-year-old attacks with 3.3km remaining on gradual 15km climb, holds 12-second gap over world-class chasers
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: dropped by BUCHMANN/EVENEPOEL/CICCONE group despite being protected GC contender, loses half-minute on relatively moderate climb
